alignments.pub_claim_text: A polynomial-time approximation algorithm for a 0-1 quadratic programming problem is designed to address the computational infeasibility of naive approaches. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same contribution (a polynomial-time approximation algorithm for the 0-1 quadratic problem) with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our novel bandit algorithms for the top-k selection problem run in polynomial time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed bandit algorithm achieves a computation time of O(log K), resulting in an exponential speedup over linear bandit algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a more specific and stronger runtime characterization (O(log K)) than the preprint's 'polynomial time', clarifying and amplifying the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments confirm the validity of our theoretical analysis in terms of both computation time and sample complexity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on large-scale data sets with more than 10^10 super arms demonstrate the superiority of the proposed algorithms in computation time and sample complexity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports experiments at very large scale and claims superiority, which clarifies the empirical support and broadens its applicability compared to the preprint statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed regularization approach allows for training temporally stable CNNs without the need for video material or expensive motion estimation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed regularization approach allows for training temporally stable CNNs without the need for video material or expensive motion estimation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological claim with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The training strategy leads to large improvements in temporal smoothness of CNNs when applied to video sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The training strategy leads to large improvements in temporal smoothness of CNNs applied to video sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about improved temporal smoothness is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For small datasets, the regularization can significantly boost generalization performance compared to naive augmentation strategies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For small datasets, the regularization can significantly boost generalization performance compared to naïve augmentation strategies. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same empirical claim and qualification about small datasets and comparison to naive augmentations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Clue Guided Copy Network for Question Generation (CGC-QG) significantly improves the performance of question generation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Clue Guided Copy Network for Question Generation (CGC-QG) significantly improves the performance of question generation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance improvement claim verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CGC-QG out-performs all previous state-of-the-art neural question generation models by a substantial margin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CGC-QG out-performs all previous state-of-the-art neural question generation models by a substantial margin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the comparative performance claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model employs a multi-task labeling strategy to guide the learning of boundaries between copying and generation in question words. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model employs a multi-task labeling strategy to guide the learning of boundaries between copying and generation in question words. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is presented unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clue word predictor uses Graph Convolutional Networks on a syntactic dependency tree to predict clue words based on their context. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clue word predictor uses Graph Convolutional Networks on a syntactic dependency tree to predict clue words based on their context. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the architectural detail and method without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum neurons can serve as a building block for quantum feed-forward neural networks capable of universal quantum computation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a truly quantum analogue of classical neurons that forms quantum feedforward neural networks capable of universal quantum computation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the central contribution (quantum neurons enabling universal quantum computation) with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method allows for fast optimisation of quantum neural networks with reduced memory requirements. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method allows for fast optimisation with reduced memory requirements, as the number of qudits required scales with only the width. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication links the fast optimisation and reduced memory claims to a specific scaling law (qudits scale with width), which clarifies the mechanism behind the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of qudits required for the quantum neural networks scales with only the width, facilitating the optimisation of deep networks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method allows for fast optimisation with reduced memory requirements, as the number of qudits required scales with only the width. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the same scaling law; it also presents this as part of the optimisation claim and with comparable or higher stated certainty, amplifying the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Benchmarking of the proposal for learning an unknown unitary shows remarkable generalisation behaviour and robustness to noisy training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We benchmark our proposal for the quantum task of learning an unknown unitary and find remarkable generalisation behaviour. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication separates the benchmarking results: it retains the generalisation claim (Q2) but reports it with reduced certainty; robustness to noise is stated separately (Q3), so the original combined high-certainty claim is tempered in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed CNN denoising method provides a simple and efficient solution for seismic data interpolation despite the lack of geophysical training labels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNN-POCS method provides a simple and efficient way to address the scarcity of geophysical training labels required by deep learning methods.
alignments.rationale: Texts are effectively the same claim about addressing scarcity of geophysical labels; wording is slightly different but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrates high generalizability and reduces the need for problem-specific training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates high generalizability and reduces the necessity of problem-specific training.
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Primary results indicate that the CNN-POCS method shows promising interpolation performances in terms of signal-to-noise ratio, de-aliasing, and weak-feature reconstruction compared to traditional methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The primary results show promising interpolation performances of the CNN-POCS method in terms of signal-to-noise ratio, dealiasing, and weak-feature reconstruction.
alignments.rationale: Result phrasing is effectively identical, listing the same performance aspects.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNN-POCS method is flexible and applicable for different types of missing traces due to its plug-and-play nature.
alignments.rationale: This claim about plug-and-play flexibility appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SCAD estimator demonstrates superiority over the $\text{l}_1$ estimator in a wide parameter range despite the nonconvex nature of the penalty leading to solution multiplicity in certain regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SCAD estimator demonstrates superiority in reconstruction performance compared to other methods across a wide parameter range.
alignments.rationale: Publication makes a more general superiority claim ("other methods" rather than explicitly l1), broadening the scope while retaining the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The global minimum of the mean square error between the estimator and the true signal is located in the replica symmetric phase.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multiplicity of solutions in the nonconvex penalty framework is connected to replica symmetry breaking in spin-glass theory.
alignments.rationale: Preprint gives a specific location of the MSE global minimum in RS phase; publication emphasizes connection between multiplicity and replica symmetry breaking, shifting the emphasis and lowering certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approximate formula for computing the cross-validation error is only applicable to the unique solution region and tends to be unstable in the multiple solution region.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An approximate formula for computing cross-validation error is developed, which is applicable to non-i.i.d. design matrices but unstable in the multiple solution region.
alignments.rationale: Publication keeps the instability caveat but clarifies and broadens applicability (non-i.i.d. matrices) and presents the contribution with higher confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed nonconvexity annealing procedure efficiently obtains the solution path.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed nonconvexity annealing procedure efficiently obtains the solution path and addresses the characteristic behavior of the annealed solution in the multiple solution region.
alignments.rationale: Publication adds that the procedure addresses behavior in the multiple-solution region, clarifying and slightly broadening the claimed capabilities and increasing confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical simulations on simulated datasets verify the consistency of the theoretical results and the efficiency of the approximate formula.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical simulations on both simulated and real-world datasets confirm the consistency of the theoretical results and the efficiency of the proposed methods.
alignments.rationale: Publication extends validation to real-world datasets (broadening) but retains the same conclusion about consistency and efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed functional representation of images using a hypernetwork allows for continuous inspection of images at various resolutions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed functional representation of images using a hypernetwork allows for continuous inspection of images at various resolutions.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about continuous multi-resolution inspection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representation exhibits properties characteristic of generative models as demonstrated through interpolation inspections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation exhibits properties characteristic of generative models as demonstrated through interpolation inspections.
alignments.rationale: Same claim about generative-model-like properties via interpolation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mechanism applied to the image super-resolution problem yields results comparable to existing super-resolution methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mechanism applied to the image super-resolution problem yields results comparable to existing super-resolution methods.
alignments.rationale: Direct restatement of the same experimental result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Distributed calibration based on consensus optimization mitigates the effect of model incompleteness in radio interferometric data processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Distributed calibration based on consensus optimization mitigates the effect of model incompleteness in radio interferometric data processing.
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same conclusion about mitigation of model incompleteness via distributed calibration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The smallest eigenvalue of the Jacobian of the mapping between input uncalibrated data and output residual data is a reliable indicator of the statistical performance of calibration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The eigenspectrum of the Jacobian of the mapping between input and output data serves as a direct indicator of the statistical performance of calibration.
alignments.rationale: Publication generalizes the indicator from the smallest eigenvalue to the full eigenspectrum, broadening the diagnostic while preserving the core idea.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis developed in this paper can be applied to other data processing steps in radio interferometry and to various machine learning problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis developed in this paper is applicable to other data processing steps in radio interferometry and various machine learning problems.
alignments.rationale: Direct match: the paper states applicability beyond the specific calibration problem as in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The statistical performance of direction-dependent distributed calibration can be studied by analyzing the distortion caused by calibration on the residual statistics.
alignments.rationale: Publication explicitly frames performance-study approach in terms of residual-statistics distortion; this specific framing was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An analytical relationship for the influence of input uncalibrated data on output residual data has been derived.
alignments.rationale: The derivation of an explicit analytical relationship appears in the publication but was not claimed in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The atomistic structure learning algorithm (ASLA) utilizes a convolutional neural network to build 2D compounds and layered structures atom by atom.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The atomistic structure learning algorithm (ASLA) utilizes a convolutional neural network to build 2D structures and planar compounds atom by atom.
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the same method description with minor wording change (planar compounds vs layered structures).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ASLA takes no prior data or knowledge on atomic interactions but inquires a first-principles quantum mechanical program for physical properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ASLA takes no prior data or knowledge on atomic interactions but inquires a first-principles quantum mechanical program for thermodynamical stability.
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies the queried property as thermodynamical stability rather than the broader "physical properties," narrowing and clarifying the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm accumulates knowledge of chemical compound space and learns the blueprint for the optimal structural arrangement of atoms for a given target property.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm accumulates knowledge of chemical compound space using reinforcement learning and stores this in the neural network.
alignments.rationale: Publication clarifies the mechanism (reinforcement learning and stored network knowledge) but phrases the claim less strongly about learning a blueprint, and its certainty is lower.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ASLA is demonstrated to work on diverse problems, including grain boundaries in graphene sheets, organic compound formation, and a surface oxide structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ASLA is demonstrated to work on diverse problems, including grain boundaries in graphene sheets, organic compound formation, and a surface oxide structure.
alignments.rationale: Identical demonstration claims in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach to structure prediction is a first step toward direct manipulation of atoms with artificially intelligent first principles computer codes.
alignments.rationale: The forward-looking statement about enabling direct manipulation of atoms is present in the preprint but omitted from the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Presentation Attacks (PAs) pose a severe threat to the security of biometric systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Presentation attacks pose a severe threat to the security of biometric systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level conclusion with equivalent meaning and emphasis; no scope or confidence shift is apparent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed fingerprint Presentation Attack Detection (PAD) scheme demonstrates a detection equal error rate (D-EER) as low as 1.36%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fingerprint presentation attack detection scheme demonstrates a detection equal error rate (D-EER) as low as 1.35%.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation of the approach was conducted on a database comprising over 4700 samples from 562 different subjects and 35 different presentation attack instrument species.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach is evaluated on a database comprising over 4700 samples from 562 different subjects and 35 different presentation attack instrument species.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same dataset description verbatim; there is no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel PAD scheme utilizes a new capture device that acquires images within the short wave infrared (SWIR) spectrum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel capture device used in the scheme is able to acquire images within the short wave infrared (SWIR) spectrum.
alignments.rationale: The core technical contribution is the same, but the publication expresses the claim with higher certainty (certainty increased to high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scientific literature constitutes a dynamic heterogeneous attributed network (HAN) that generates a vast amount of data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scientific literature constitutes a dynamic heterogeneous attributed network (HAN) that generates a vast amount of data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The tool Peerus offers real-time recommendations of newly published papers and facilitates the search for active experts for collaborations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The tool Peerus offers real-time recommendations of newly published papers and facilitates the search for active experts for collaborations.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim about the tool Peerus; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most works on attributed network embedding do not adequately consider textual attributes or leverage recent natural language processing techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most works on attributed network embedding do not adequately consider textual attributes or leverage recent natural language processing techniques.
alignments.rationale: The limitation is carried over verbatim into the publication; no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing methods for jointly learning node and document representations fail to effectively infer representations for new documents lacking network information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing methods for jointly learning node and document representations fail to effectively infer representations for new documents lacking network information.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The relationship between textual and graph data in text-attributed heterogeneous networks is an open research direction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The relationship between textual and graph data in text-attributed heterogeneous networks is an open research direction.
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement appears unchanged in content and emphasis in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm learns a similarity measure between documents from the network topology of a structured corpus.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm learns a similarity measure between documents from the network topology of a structured corpus.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mutual attention mechanism is designed using the Scaled Dot-Product Attention.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mutual attention mechanism is designed using the Scaled Dot-Product Attention.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Preliminary experiment results demonstrate the capacity of the model to learn a meaningful textual similarity.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result claim is carried over without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Oracle inequalities with fast and slow rates are derived for the analysis estimator.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The theory is extended to the square root analysis estimator.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Constant-friendly rates are obtained for (square root) total variation regularized estimators on graphs, matching previous results up to log terms.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same technical result, restating it with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An oracle inequality for the (square root) total variation regularized estimator over the cycle graph is obtained.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An oracle inequality is obtained for the (square root) total variation regularized estimator over the cycle graph.
alignments.rationale: The specific oracle inequality over the cycle graph is stated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The communication time of distributed optimization algorithms is influenced by the algorithm's convergence properties, the compression scheme, and the transmission rate of the digital channel.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The communication time of distributed optimization algorithms is influenced by the algorithm's convergence properties, the compression scheme, and the transmission rate of the digital channel.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same multi-factor characterization of communication time; content and emphasis are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to design quantizers for linearly convergent distributed algorithms that compress communicated information to a few bits while preserving linear convergence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to design quantizers for linearly convergent distributed algorithms that compress communicated information to a few bits while preserving linear convergence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The communication time of distributed algorithms can be characterized as a function of the available transmission rate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The communication time of distributed algorithms can be characterized as a function of the available transmission rate.
alignments.rationale: The result about characterizing communication time by transmission rate appears unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A co-design of machine learning and communication protocols is necessary for effective machine learning over networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A co-design of machine learning and communication protocols is necessary for effective machine learning over networks.
alignments.rationale: The concluding recommendation for co-design is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Different communication structures, such as decentralized and fully distributed algorithms, affect the performance of learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level claim about the impact of communication structures does not appear in the preprint and thus is a new (added) claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A generative model can learn compact latent codes that efficiently represent and reconstruct a video sequence from its missing or under-sampled measurements. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We use a low-rank constraint on the latent codes to impose a low-dimensional manifold model on the generated video sequence. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the idea specifically as imposing a low-rank (low-dimensional manifold) constraint on latent codes, which is a more specific/clarified formulation of the preprint's broader claim about compact latent codes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: If a video sequence belongs to the range of the pretrained generative network, it can be recovered by estimating the underlying compact latent codes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate the conditional claim that in-range sequences can be recovered solely by estimating latent codes; no matching statement appears in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Even if a video sequence does not belong to the range of a pretrained network, the true video sequence can still be recovered by jointly updating the latent codes and the weights of the generative network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a method for jointly updating the weights of the generator and latent codes while recovering a video sequence from compressive measurements. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same joint-update recovery approach but frames it as a proposed method with more moderate certainty, so the claim is clarified/tempered rather than removed or contradicted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recovery problem is regularized by imposing low-rank and similarity constraints on the latent codes of the neighboring frames in the video sequence to avoid overfitting. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We use a low-rank constraint on the latent codes to impose a low-dimensional manifold model on the generated video sequence. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the low-rank regularization aspect but omits mention of similarity constraints and the explicit overfitting rationale, narrowing and clarifying the original regularization claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Missing frames in a video sequence can be generated by interpolating the latent codes of the observed frames in the low-dimensional space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not mention interpolation of latent codes to generate missing frames; this specific result is not present in the publication's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed wide-baseline omnidirectional stereo algorithm computes dense depth estimates from fisheye images using a deep convolutional neural network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed wide-baseline omnidirectional stereo algorithm computes dense depth estimates from fisheye images using a deep convolutional neural network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this technical contribution verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach generates one dense omnidirectional depth map with full 360-degree coverage at the rig global coordinate system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach generates one dense omnidirectional depth map with full 360° coverage at the rig global coordinate system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result identically, so no change is observed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments show that the proposed algorithm outperforms conventional depth estimation methods and generates highly accurate depth maps. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments show that the proposed algorithm outperforms conventional depth estimation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the 'outperforms conventional methods' portion but omits the standalone statement 'generates highly accurate depth maps' in this claim (that element appears instead as a separate claim about datasets), so the original compound claim is clarified/narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed cascaded RNN model using gated recurrent units (GRUs) effectively explores the redundant and complementary information of hyperspectral images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed cascaded RNN model using gated recurrent units effectively explores the redundant and complementary information of hyperspectral images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this contribution directly and without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first RNN layer in the proposed model eliminates redundant information between adjacent spectral bands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first RNN layer in the proposed model eliminates redundant information between adjacent spectral bands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This layer-level claim appears in the publication unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second RNN layer in the proposed model learns complementary information from non-adjacent spectral bands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The second RNN layer in the proposed model learns complementary information from nonadjacent spectral bands. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim (difference is only orthography), so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed models achieve better results than the compared models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed models achieve better results than the compared models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical superiority claim is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep sparse auto-encoders with mixed structure regularization have the potential to improve unsupervised abnormality detection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep sparse autoencoders with MSR have the potential to improve unsupervised abnormality detection using deep learning compared to common deep autoencoders. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same overall potential but frames it more precisely (MSR vs. common deep autoencoders) and with lower stated certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unsupervised abnormality detection using deep sparse auto-encoders requires only healthy data for training. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not state or emphasize the training-data-only-healthy requirement; this specific methodological assertion is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mixed structure regularization has the potential to improve overall performance by 20-30% compared to deep sparse and denoising auto-encoders in detecting coronary artery disease from CCTA. (certainty=low, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SDAE-MSR achieved the best aggregated area under the curve (AUC) with a 20% improvement and the best aggregated Average Precision (AP) with a 30% improvement upon the SAE and SDAE. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same numeric improvements but with higher certainty and explicit metrics, amplifying the confidence in the originally tentative preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The missing entries in undersampled data cubes can be accurately imputed using non-convex techniques from sparse approximation and matrix completion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The missing entries in the under-sampled data cube can be accurately imputed using non-convex techniques from sparse approximation and matrix completion. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this imputation claim directly and without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The peak signal-to-noise ratio can typically be improved by 2 to 5 dB over current state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The peak signal-to-noise ratio can typically be improved by 2 dB to 5 dB over current state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric PSNR improvement claim is reproduced identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The improvements in peak signal-to-noise ratio were observed when simulating a p=16 mosaic sensor measuring both high and low altitude urban and rural scenes as well as ground-based scenes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The improvements in peak signal-to-noise ratio were observed when simulating a p = 16 mosaic sensor measuring both high and low altitude urban and rural scenes as well as ground-based scenes. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same experimental context and observations as the preprint, so this claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A matrix factorization approach for network embedding, inspired by GloVe, better handles non co-occurrence with a competitive time-complexity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A matrix factorization approach for network embedding, inspired by GloVe, better handles non co-occurrence with a competitive time-complexity. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The two texts are essentially identical in content and emphasis, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our model achieves state-of-the-art performance in network embedding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model achieves state-of-the-art performance in quantitative evaluations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the qualifier 'in quantitative evaluations', clarifying the evaluation context without changing the substantive claim of state-of-the-art performance.
